services.name: WalletService
services.comment:
WalletService exposes a simplified, swap-vocabulary-free wallet API on top
of the running daemon. It composes the underlying swap subsystem, ark VTXO
operations, boarding deposits, cooperative leave (VTXO-to-onchain), and
unilateral exit behind one small surface: the seven core verbs that map
1:1 to what a user actually does day-to-day - create, unlock, send, recv,
list, balance, exit - plus a few additional methods (Deposit, Status,
SubscribeWallet) used internally and by recv --onchain. The service is
registered only when the daemon is built with the wavewalletrpc build tag
(which also requires swapruntime).

services.name: WalletInspectionService
services.comment:
WalletInspectionService exposes technical drill-downs for wallet activity.
Unlike WalletService.List, this surface may include internal correlators and
ledger rows so operators can explain how a user-facing entry was executed.

methods.name: Create
methods.comment:
Create initializes a new wallet from a freshly generated aezeed
mnemonic. The daemon generates the seed, encrypts it with the
supplied password, and returns the mnemonic so the caller can record
it. For recovery flows the caller MAY supply an existing mnemonic in
the request; in that case the same mnemonic is echoed back. Proxies
waverpc.GenSeed + waverpc.InitWallet server-side.

methods.name: Unlock
methods.comment:
Unlock decrypts the on-disk wallet seed using the supplied password
and starts the wallet subsystem. Proxies waverpc.UnlockWallet.

methods.name: PrepareSend
methods.comment:
PrepareSend validates and previews an outbound payment without
moving funds. The response carries a short-lived send_intent_id
that must be consumed by Send.

methods.name: Send
methods.comment:
Send dispatches a previously prepared outbound payment. The
request is intentionally intent-only: callers must use
PrepareSend first so they can present amount, rail, fee, and
total-outflow details before funds move.

methods.name: Recv
methods.comment:
Recv asks the daemon for a Lightning invoice the caller can hand
out. Internally the daemon sets up the inbound receive via its
owned swap subsystem; the invoice is signed with a daemon-managed
key, not an ephemeral process-local key.

methods.name: List
methods.comment:
List returns the unified wallet view selected by ListRequest.view:
ACTIVITY (default) is the merged WalletEntry stream; VTXOS is the
live VTXO inventory; ONCHAIN is the boarding-plus-sweep on-chain
history. The body oneof on ListResponse discriminates the typed
result so agents see a tagged union, not a polymorphic blob.

methods.name: Deposit
methods.comment:
Deposit returns a boarding onchain address the caller can fund. The
daemon rolls the boarding output into a VTXO during the next round.
Surfaced internally and via `recv --onchain`; not a top-level CLI
verb.

methods.name: Balance
methods.comment:
Balance returns the unified balance across confirmed VTXOs and
in-flight inbound and outbound amounts.

methods.name: Status
methods.comment:
Status returns a wallet-level readiness summary: daemon readiness,
wallet-unlocked state, balance summary, pending-entry count. Kept in
the proto for programmatic callers; not surfaced as a CLI verb (the
`getinfo` CLI verb covers the human-facing readiness view).

methods.name: GetExitPlan
methods.comment:
GetExitPlan previews unilateral-exit readiness for one VTXO. The
response includes CPFP fee input requirements and, when funding is
needed, a backing-wallet address callers can fund before forced unroll.

methods.name: SweepWallet
methods.comment:
SweepWallet previews or broadcasts a normal backing-wallet sweep to a
caller-supplied Bitcoin address. This sweeps wallet-managed funds left
after CPFP/change/unroll proceeds and does not sweep boarding outputs.

methods.name: Exit
methods.comment:
Exit queues a cooperative leave for the specified VTXO outpoint by
default. When the caller supplies force_unroll_ack exactly, the daemon
starts a unilateral unroll instead after checking that the outpoint is
present in the local backing wallet's UTXO set.

methods.name: ExitStatus
methods.comment:
ExitStatus reports the current phase of an unroll job for the
specified VTXO outpoint, including recovery chain progress and
sweep state. Proxies waverpc.GetUnrollStatus.

methods.name: ExitSummary
methods.comment:
ExitSummary reports the wallet-wide portfolio of in-progress exits:
one row per active exit plus aggregate totals for the amount still
being recovered, the estimated fees, and the estimated net recoverable.

methods.name: SubscribeWallet
methods.comment:
SubscribeWallet streams activity updates as they happen. The stream
is resumable: each response carries a monotonic cursor the client can
reconnect from to replay everything after it without gaps.

methods.name: InspectActivity
methods.comment: InspectActivity returns a technical trace for one WalletEntry id.

CreateRequest.name: CreateRequest
CreateRequest.comment:
CreateRequest carries the parameters for WalletService.Create: the
encryption password, optional seed material, and recovery options.

fields.name: wallet_password
fields.comment:
wallet_password is the password used to encrypt the on-disk seed.
Must be at least 8 bytes. The password is consumed and zeroed by
the daemon after wallet initialization.

fields.name: seed_passphrase
fields.comment:
seed_passphrase is the optional aezeed passphrase (BIP39-style
25th-word) protecting the mnemonic itself. Distinct from
wallet_password.

fields.name: mnemonic
fields.comment:
mnemonic is the 24-word aezeed mnemonic to import. Empty means
"generate a fresh seed"; non-empty means "recover from this
mnemonic". When non-empty the response echoes the same mnemonic
back unchanged.

fields.name: recover_state
fields.comment:
recover_state asks the daemon to scan deterministic Ark wallet keys
after importing mnemonic and rebuild local Ark state from chain and
indexer data.

fields.name: recovery_window
fields.comment:
recovery_window is the number of key indexes to scan per recovery key
family. Zero uses the daemon wallet.recoverywindow config value.

CreateResponse.name: CreateResponse
CreateResponse.comment:
CreateResponse returns the wallet mnemonic and identity, plus a summary of
any Ark-state recovery performed during Create.

fields.name: mnemonic
fields.comment:
mnemonic is the 24-word aezeed mnemonic for the wallet. For fresh
wallets (request mnemonic empty) this is the newly generated
seed and the caller MUST persist it offline before any unlock
sequence - losing it makes the wallet unrecoverable. For recovery
flows (request mnemonic supplied) this is the same mnemonic echoed
back for confirmation.

fields.name: identity_pubkey
fields.comment:
identity_pubkey is the hex-encoded daemon wallet identity public
key derived from the newly created wallet.

fields.name: recovery_ran
fields.comment: recovery_ran is true when Create attempted Ark-state recovery.

fields.name: recovered_boarding_addresses
fields.comment:
recovered_boarding_addresses is the number of boarding addresses
rebuilt and persisted from deterministic keys.

fields.name: recovered_boarding_utxos
fields.comment:
recovered_boarding_utxos is the number of confirmed wallet UTXOs found
at recovered boarding addresses during the recovery scan.

fields.name: recovered_vtxos
fields.comment:
recovered_vtxos is the number of indexed VTXOs restored into local
wallet state.

fields.name: recovered_oor_receive_scripts
fields.comment:
recovered_oor_receive_scripts is the number of registered OOR receive
scripts matched and restored into local ownership metadata.

fields.name: recovered_oor_events
fields.comment:
recovered_oor_events is the number of OOR recipient events processed
during recovery.

UnlockRequest.name: UnlockRequest
UnlockRequest.comment:
UnlockRequest carries the password used to decrypt the on-disk seed for
WalletService.Unlock.

fields.name: wallet_password
fields.comment: wallet_password is the password used to decrypt the on-disk seed.

UnlockResponse.name: UnlockResponse
UnlockResponse.comment:
UnlockResponse returns the identity of the wallet unlocked by
WalletService.Unlock.

fields.name: identity_pubkey
fields.comment:
identity_pubkey is the hex-encoded daemon wallet identity public
key of the unlocked wallet.

PrepareSendRequest.name: PrepareSendRequest
PrepareSendRequest.comment:
PrepareSendRequest describes the outbound payment to validate and preview
via WalletService.PrepareSend, before any funds move.

fields.name: invoice
fields.comment:
invoice is a BOLT-11 Lightning invoice. The daemon pays it via
its owned swap subsystem; the swap server picks same-Ark p2p vs
real Lightning transparently.

fields.name: onchain_address
fields.comment:
onchain_address is a bech32 onchain destination. The daemon
submits a LeaveVTXOs request covering amt_sat plus fees.

fields.name: amt_sat
fields.comment:
amt_sat is required for onchain sends. For onchain sends amt_sat
must be strictly positive unless sweep_all is set, in which case
it must be zero. For invoice sends amt_sat is ignored: v1 requires
an amount-bearing BOLT-11 invoice; amountless invoices are rejected
at the wallet layer until plumbing for caller-supplied amounts
lands in the swap subserver.

fields.name: note
fields.comment:
note is an optional caller-supplied label persisted alongside the
entry. It is never interpreted by the daemon.

fields.name: max_fee_sat
fields.comment:
max_fee_sat is the optional caller cap on routing or sweep fees.
Zero means use daemon defaults.

fields.name: sweep_all
fields.comment:
sweep_all signals an explicit wallet-emptying onchain send: every
live VTXO is swept to the destination, less fees. The caller must
set amt_sat = 0 when this flag is true and a strictly positive
amt_sat otherwise. This makes "drain the wallet" structurally
distinct from "amt_sat defaulted to zero" so a typo cannot empty
the wallet by accident. Ignored on the invoice path.

PrepareSendResponse.name: PrepareSendResponse
PrepareSendResponse.comment:
PrepareSendResponse is the preview of a prepared send: the single-use
intent token plus the amount, fee, rail, and quote detail a caller should
confirm before calling Send.

fields.name: send_intent_id
fields.comment: send_intent_id is a short-lived, single-use token consumed by Send.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment: amount_sat is the destination principal amount.

fields.name: expected_fee_sat
fields.comment: expected_fee_sat is meaningful only when fee_known is true.

fields.name: fee_known
fields.comment: fee_known is false when a required remote quote API is missing.

fields.name: expected_total_outflow_sat
fields.comment:
expected_total_outflow_sat is meaningful only when
total_outflow_known is true.

fields.name: total_outflow_known
fields.comment:
total_outflow_known is false when the final outflow depends on a
remote quote that is not available yet.

fields.name: rail
fields.comment:
rail is the expected settlement rail for this prepared send. Onchain
prepares always return SEND_RAIL_ONCHAIN. Invoice prepares map the
swap quote settlement type when a remote quote is available; when
no quote is available (LOCAL_ONLY) or the quote does not
distinguish same-Ark from Lightning, the value is
SEND_RAIL_OFFCHAIN_UNKNOWN until Send starts.

fields.name: quote_status
fields.comment:
quote_status describes how complete the prepare-time quote is.
Invoice sends: COMPLETE when every amount and fee is backed by a
swap remote quote; LOCAL_ONLY when the invoice parsed locally but
the swap quote API was unavailable. Onchain sends: COMPLETE when
waverpc.EstimateFee returned a binding operator quote;
LOCAL_ONLY when only a local fee floor could be computed.

fields.name: destination_summary
fields.comment:
destination_summary is a short display string suitable for CLI and
UI confirmation prompts.

fields.name: invoice_description
fields.comment: invoice_description is the BOLT-11 description when present.

fields.name: payment_hash
fields.comment: payment_hash is the invoice payment hash when available.

fields.name: expires_at_unix
fields.comment:
expires_at_unix is the unix timestamp after which Send rejects the
prepared intent.

fields.name: selected_outpoints
fields.comment:
selected_outpoints is populated for onchain sends so Send can spend
exactly the VTXO set previewed to the user.

fields.name: warning
fields.comment:
warning carries a concise human-facing caveat for local-only
previews.

fields.name: credit_preview
fields.comment:
credit_preview is populated when the invoice send will or can use
sat-native server credits.

CreditPreview.name: CreditPreview
CreditPreview.comment:
CreditPreview describes how server-side sat-native credits factor into a
prepared send. It is populated on PrepareSendResponse when the invoice
send will or can draw on credits.

fields.name: must_use_credit
fields.comment:
must_use_credit is true when the invoice amount cannot be
represented by the normal vHTLC path and the send must be settled
from the credit balance.

fields.name: credit_applied_sat
fields.comment:
credit_applied_sat is the credit balance in satoshis the quote
expects to reserve for this send.

fields.name: credit_shortfall_sat
fields.comment:
credit_shortfall_sat is the additional credit in satoshis needed
before this payment can be admitted.

fields.name: credit_topup_sat
fields.comment:
credit_topup_sat is the Ark top-up amount in satoshis required to
cover the shortfall. It is rounded up and dust-limited by the server.

fields.name: ark_funding_sat
fields.comment:
ark_funding_sat is the amount in satoshis the client must still fund
through the normal vHTLC path.

SendRequest.name: SendRequest
SendRequest.comment:
SendRequest dispatches a previously prepared send by consuming the
single-use intent token returned by PrepareSend.

fields.name: send_intent_id
fields.comment: send_intent_id is returned by PrepareSend and may be consumed once.

SendResponse.name: SendResponse
SendResponse.comment:
SendResponse returns the initial WalletEntry for a dispatched send and the
actual amount that will leave the wallet.

fields.name: entry
fields.comment:
entry is the initial WalletEntry persisted for this send. The
caller can poll List or SubscribeWallet for subsequent status
transitions.

fields.name: actual_amount_sat
fields.comment:
actual_amount_sat is the real amount that will leave the wallet
for this operation. For invoice sends it matches the caller's
amt_sat (or the invoice amount for amount-bearing invoices). For a
bounded onchain send it matches the requested amt_sat: the
seal-time fee handshake returns a change VTXO rather than sweeping
whole VTXOs. For a sweep_all onchain send it reflects the SUM of
the swept VTXOs (the whole-wallet drain). CLI and UI surfaces
SHOULD echo this back to the user before treating the send as
confirmed.

WalletEntry.name: WalletEntry
WalletEntry.comment:
WalletEntry is the single flat row type returned by the ACTIVITY view of
List and by SubscribeWallet. The shape is deliberately minimal: display
clients should be able to render kind/status/amount/fee/phase directly
without parsing daemon-specific swap or ledger internals. Power-user detail
belongs in WalletInspectionService.InspectActivity.

fields.name: id
fields.comment:
id is the stable identifier for this entry. Swap-backed SEND and
RECV rows use the Lightning payment_hash. Credit-backed RECV rows
use the credit operation id (see CreditReceive.operation_id); the
payment_hash lives on recv progress/detail instead. EXIT rows use
leave_job_id (or sweep txid); DEPOSIT rows use the boarding
outpoint or txid.

fields.name: kind
fields.comment: kind is the user-visible category.

fields.name: status
fields.comment:
status is the coarse user-facing outcome: PENDING, COMPLETE, or
FAILED. It mirrors the backing source's durable state and should
not be reinterpreted by display clients.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment:
amount_sat is signed: positive values are incoming to the wallet,
negative values are outgoing.

fields.name: fee_sat
fields.comment:
fee_sat is the absolute fee paid (or pending) for this
operation.

fields.name: counterparty
fields.comment:
counterparty is a short, display-friendly identifier of the other
side: a truncated invoice for Lightning, an ark peer address for
OOR, a bech32 onchain address for exits, or "boarding" for
deposits.

fields.name: created_at_unix
fields.comment: created_at_unix is the local creation timestamp in unix seconds.

fields.name: updated_at_unix
fields.comment:
updated_at_unix is the last persisted update timestamp in unix
seconds. List ACTIVITY and SubscribeWallet sort by this field,
descending.

fields.name: note
fields.comment:
note is the caller-supplied label captured at submit time, if
any.

fields.name: failure_reason
fields.comment:
failure_reason is populated only when status is FAILED. It is a
single human-readable string, never a coded enum tree.

fields.name: request
fields.comment:
request is the original payment request or destination associated
with the entry, when the backing subsystem persists it. This is
useful for expanded views and inspection; compact clients should
not need to parse it to explain status or lifecycle.

fields.name: progress
fields.comment:
progress carries lifecycle-specific metadata already normalized by the
wallet service. Clients can render phase_label directly or switch on
phase without inferring meaning from swap/ledger internals.

fields.name: failure_code
fields.comment:
failure_code is a stable, machine-readable classification of why a
FAILED entry failed. It is set ONLY on FAILED entries; a non-failed
entry omits the field entirely (presence-tracked), so its absence is the
canonical "no failure" signal and ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_UNSPECIFIED is never
sent on the wire. failure_reason remains the human-readable supplement.

WalletEntryRequest.name: WalletEntryRequest
WalletEntryRequest.comment:
WalletEntryRequest describes the user-recognizable request that created a
WalletEntry. It is a oneof because each operation is backed by exactly one
request shape.

fields.name: lightning_invoice
fields.comment:
lightning_invoice is populated for Lightning send and receive
entries.

fields.name: onchain_address
fields.comment: onchain_address is populated for deposit and exit entries.

fields.name: ark_address
fields.comment: ark_address is populated for direct Ark send or receive entries.

LightningInvoiceRequest.name: LightningInvoiceRequest
LightningInvoiceRequest.comment:
LightningInvoiceRequest captures the Lightning request associated with an
activity entry.

fields.name: invoice
fields.comment: invoice is the BOLT-11 payment request.

fields.name: payment_hash
fields.comment:
payment_hash identifies the invoice and remains stable after the
invoice itself is no longer convenient to display.

OnchainAddressRequest.name: OnchainAddressRequest
OnchainAddressRequest.comment:
OnchainAddressRequest captures the onchain address associated with an
activity entry.

fields.name: address
fields.comment: address is the bech32 onchain address originally issued or targeted.

ArkAddressRequest.name: ArkAddressRequest
ArkAddressRequest.comment: ArkAddressRequest captures the Ark address associated with an activity entry.

fields.name: address
fields.comment: address is the Ark address originally issued or targeted.

WalletEntryProgress.name: WalletEntryProgress
WalletEntryProgress.comment:
WalletEntryProgress carries lifecycle metadata normalized by the wallet
service.

fields.name: phase
fields.comment: phase is the coarse lifecycle phase for this entry.

fields.name: phase_label
fields.comment:
phase_label is a short lowercase label that clients can render as-is
while they migrate to enum-based presentation.

fields.name: payment_hash
fields.comment: payment_hash is populated for Lightning-backed send/recv entries.

fields.name: txid
fields.comment: txid is populated when the backing ledger row has an onchain txid.

fields.name: confirmation_height
fields.comment: confirmation_height is populated once the source records it.

fields.name: vtxo_outpoint
fields.comment: vtxo_outpoint is populated when a swap observes the Ark vHTLC output.

fields.name: preimage
fields.comment:
preimage is the hex-encoded Lightning payment preimage once the swap
revealed it. For a completed Lightning-backed send this is the proof of
payment for the paid invoice (sha256(preimage) == payment_hash); it is
empty until the preimage is durably known and for non-Lightning entries.

RecvRequest.name: RecvRequest
RecvRequest.comment: RecvRequest describes the inbound receive to open via WalletService.Recv.

fields.name: amt_sat
fields.comment: amt_sat is the amount the caller wants to receive in satoshis.

fields.name: memo
fields.comment: memo is the optional human-readable memo to embed in the invoice.

RecvResponse.name: RecvResponse
RecvResponse.comment:
RecvResponse returns the BOLT-11 invoice to hand out and the initial
WalletEntry tracking the pending receive.

fields.name: invoice
fields.comment:
invoice is the BOLT-11 payment request the caller hands out to
the payer.

fields.name: entry
fields.comment: entry is the initial WalletEntry persisted for this receive.

fields.name: credit_receive
fields.comment:
credit_receive is populated when the receive is backed by server credits
rather than a client-claimable vHTLC.

CreditReceive.name: CreditReceive
CreditReceive.comment:
CreditReceive describes a receive backed by server credits rather than a
client-claimable vHTLC. It is populated on RecvResponse for the credit
path.

fields.name: operation_id
fields.comment:
operation_id is the durable credit-subsystem operation id tracking
this receive. It is the WalletEntry.id for credit RECV rows; the
server-owned payment_hash is in payment_hash below.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment:
amount_sat is the amount in satoshis the caller asked to receive,
credited to the wallet account once the server-owned invoice settles.

fields.name: payment_hash
fields.comment:
payment_hash is the hex-encoded payment hash of the server-owned
receive invoice.

ListRequest.name: ListRequest
ListRequest.comment: ListRequest selects the wallet view and filters for WalletService.List.

fields.name: view
fields.comment:
view selects which slice of wallet state to return. The default
(LIST_VIEW_UNSPECIFIED) is treated as LIST_VIEW_ACTIVITY.

WIRE-BREAKING CHANGE: this message intentionally renumbers the
PR #440 fields (pending_only/kinds/limit/offset). wavewalletrpc has
no deployed external consumers yet, so the cleaner shape with
`view` at field 1 is preferred over a backwards-compatible
append. Any binary built against PR #440's ListRequest must be
recompiled.

fields.name: pending_only
fields.comment:
pending_only filters the returned entries to those still in
flight. Applies to the ACTIVITY view; ignored for VTXOS and
ONCHAIN.

fields.name: kinds
fields.comment:
kinds optionally narrows the response to specific entry
categories. Applies to the ACTIVITY view; ignored for VTXOS and
ONCHAIN. When empty, all kinds are returned.

fields.name: limit
fields.comment: limit caps the response size. Zero means use the daemon default.

fields.name: offset
fields.comment:
offset is the pagination offset within the chosen view. It applies
to the VTXOS and ONCHAIN views; the ACTIVITY view paginates by the
opaque cursor below instead and ignores offset.

fields.name: cursor
fields.comment:
cursor is the opaque pagination token for the ACTIVITY view. Empty
starts from the newest entry; otherwise it is the next_cursor
returned by the previous ActivityList page. It is stable across
concurrent inserts, so paging never skips or duplicates rows.
Ignored for the VTXOS and ONCHAIN views.

ListResponse.name: ListResponse
ListResponse.comment:
ListResponse carries the typed result of WalletService.List as a oneof
discriminated by the requested view.

fields.name: activity
fields.comment:
activity is set when the request view is ACTIVITY (the default):
the merged, time-sorted WalletEntry stream.

fields.name: vtxos
fields.comment:
vtxos is set when the request view is VTXOS: the live spendable
VTXO inventory.

fields.name: onchain
fields.comment:
onchain is set when the request view is ONCHAIN: the on-chain
transaction history.

ActivityList.name: ActivityList
ActivityList.comment:
ActivityList is the ACTIVITY-view body of ListResponse: the merged,
time-sorted WalletEntry stream.

fields.name: entries
fields.comment: entries are the unified, time-sorted wallet operations.

fields.name: total
fields.comment:
total is the number of entries in this page. It is a page count,
not a full-feed count: the feed is paged by an opaque cursor, so
callers use has_more, not total, to decide whether to fetch again.

fields.name: has_more
fields.comment: has_more reports whether more entries exist after this page.

fields.name: next_cursor
fields.comment:
next_cursor is the opaque token to pass as ListRequest.cursor to
fetch the next page. Empty when has_more is false.

VTXOInventory.name: VTXOInventory
VTXOInventory.comment:
VTXOInventory is the VTXOS-view body of ListResponse: the live spendable
VTXO inventory.

fields.name: vtxos
fields.comment:
vtxos are the live spendable VTXOs in the wallet. Order is
unspecified.

fields.name: total
fields.comment: total is the count of all live VTXOs before limit/offset.

WalletVTXO.name: WalletVTXO
WalletVTXO.comment:
WalletVTXO is the wallet-facing view of one VTXO. Internal lifecycle
detail (forfeiting flow, chain depth, etc.) is hidden; power-users can
reach the full shape via `ark vtxos list`.

fields.name: outpoint
fields.comment: outpoint is the VTXO's outpoint in "txid:index" format.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment: amount_sat is the value of the VTXO in satoshis.

fields.name: status
fields.comment:
status is a short lowercase string: "live", "pending_forfeit",
"forfeiting", "spending", "unilateral_exit". Internal terminal
states (forfeited / spent / failed) are filtered out of the
wallet view.

fields.name: batch_expiry
fields.comment:
batch_expiry is the absolute block height at which the batch-level
timelock expires.

fields.name: relative_expiry
fields.comment:
relative_expiry is the CSV delay (in blocks) for the unilateral
exit path.

fields.name: commitment_txid
fields.comment:
commitment_txid is the hex-encoded txid of the on-chain commitment
transaction anchoring this VTXO's tree.

OnchainHistory.name: OnchainHistory
OnchainHistory.comment:
OnchainHistory is the ONCHAIN-view body of ListResponse: the on-chain
transaction history.

fields.name: txs
fields.comment:
txs are the on-chain transaction history rows: boarding deposits,
boarding sweeps, leave outputs, round commitment txs that touched
this wallet. Sorted newest first.

fields.name: total
fields.comment: total is the count of matching rows before limit/offset.

fields.name: has_more
fields.comment: has_more is true when another page is available.

OnchainTx.name: OnchainTx
OnchainTx.comment:
OnchainTx is the wallet-facing view of one on-chain transaction. It
flattens the daemon's richer TransactionHistoryEntry shape to the fields
a wallet user actually needs.

fields.name: txid
fields.comment:
txid is the hex-encoded transaction id, when known. Some ledger
rows (e.g. fee_paid) do not carry a txid; those entries surface
with txid="" and the row is still informative.

fields.name: kind
fields.comment:
kind is the high-level transaction type: "boarding", "sweep",
"round", "oor", or "fee".

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment:
amount_sat is the signed transaction amount in satoshis. Positive
values are credits to this wallet; negative values are debits.

fields.name: fee_sat
fields.comment:
fee_sat is the absolute fee paid (or pending) for this
transaction, when known.

fields.name: status
fields.comment:
status is a short lowercase string: "confirmed", "pending",
"recorded", or a backing-specific lifecycle value.

fields.name: confirmation_height
fields.comment:
confirmation_height is set when the source records a chain
confirmation height.

fields.name: created_at_unix
fields.comment: created_at_unix is the local creation timestamp in unix seconds.

fields.name: description
fields.comment: description is a human-readable local note.

DepositRequest.name: DepositRequest
DepositRequest.comment: DepositRequest asks WalletService.Deposit for a fresh boarding address.

fields.name: amt_sat_hint
fields.comment:
amt_sat_hint is an optional caller hint about the expected deposit
amount. The daemon may use it to size internal accounting; the
returned boarding address is not amount-bound.

DepositResponse.name: DepositResponse
DepositResponse.comment:
DepositResponse returns a fresh boarding address and the initial
DEPOSIT-kind WalletEntry tracking the pending boarding operation.

fields.name: onchain_address
fields.comment:
onchain_address is a fresh boarding address the caller should
fund. The daemon monitors it and rolls the boarding output into
the next round.

fields.name: entry
fields.comment:
entry is the initial DEPOSIT-kind WalletEntry persisted to track
the pending boarding operation.

BalanceRequest.name: BalanceRequest
BalanceRequest.comment: BalanceRequest is the empty request for WalletService.Balance.

BalanceResponse.name: BalanceResponse
BalanceResponse.comment:
BalanceResponse is the unified balance summary returned by
WalletService.Balance across confirmed, in-flight, and credit amounts.

fields.name: confirmed_sat
fields.comment: confirmed_sat is the total spendable VTXO amount in satoshis.

fields.name: pending_in_sat
fields.comment:
pending_in_sat is the total in-flight inbound amount (boarding
plus receive operations).

fields.name: pending_out_sat
fields.comment:
pending_out_sat is the total in-flight outbound amount (send plus
exit operations).

fields.name: credit_available_sat
fields.comment:
credit_available_sat is the server-authoritative available credit
balance for the wallet identity.

fields.name: credit_reserved_sat
fields.comment:
credit_reserved_sat is the server-authoritative in-flight credit
reservation amount.

StatusRequest.name: StatusRequest
StatusRequest.comment: StatusRequest is the empty request for WalletService.Status.

StatusResponse.name: StatusResponse
StatusResponse.comment:
StatusResponse is the wallet-level readiness summary returned by
WalletService.Status.

fields.name: ready
fields.comment: ready is true when the daemon and its dependencies are up.

fields.name: unlocked
fields.comment:
unlocked is a legacy wallet-exists signal: true once a wallet
seed exists on disk or is loaded in memory. Use ready to check
whether wallet RPCs are currently usable.

fields.name: network
fields.comment:
network is the bitcoin network the daemon is configured for, for
example "mainnet", "testnet", "testnet4", "signet", or
"regtest".

fields.name: balance
fields.comment: balance is the unified balance summary at the time of the call.

fields.name: pending_count
fields.comment:
pending_count is the number of WalletEntry rows in PENDING
status.

GetExitPlanRequest.name: GetExitPlanRequest
GetExitPlanRequest.comment:
GetExitPlanRequest lists the VTXO outpoints to preview for unilateral exit
via WalletService.GetExitPlan.

fields.name: outpoints
fields.comment: outpoints are the VTXO outpoints to preview, each "txid:index".

fields.name: conf_target
fields.comment:
conf_target selects the fee-estimation target in blocks. Zero uses
the daemon's default unroll confirmation target.

GetExitPlanResponse.name: GetExitPlanResponse
GetExitPlanResponse.comment:
GetExitPlanResponse returns one ExitPlanEntry per requested outpoint, plus
the batch-level fee estimate and aggregate funding verdict.

fields.name: plans
fields.comment:
plans holds one entry per requested outpoint, in request order.
Entries are evaluated against a running wallet allocation: each
feasible exit reserves its fee inputs before the next entry is
planned, so a per-entry verdict reflects the wallet left after the
earlier entries in the batch notionally start.

fields.name: fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte
fields.comment:
fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte is the shared chain fee estimate used for all
entries.

fields.name: can_start
fields.comment:
can_start is the AND over every entry that has no per-outpoint error.
Because entries draw from a shared wallet via the running allocation
above, it is true only when every requested exit can be funded
simultaneously, not merely one at a time.

fields.name: total_funding_shortfall_sat
fields.comment:
total_funding_shortfall_sat is the summed shortfall across entries
under simultaneous funding, so it already accounts for fee inputs two
outpoints would otherwise both claim.

fields.name: total_recommended_funding_sat
fields.comment:
total_recommended_funding_sat is the summed recommended funding across
entries.

ExitPlanEntry.name: ExitPlanEntry
ExitPlanEntry.comment:
ExitPlanEntry is the per-outpoint unilateral-exit readiness preview: the
backing-wallet fee requirements and, when an unroll job already exists,
its current state.

fields.name: outpoint
fields.comment: outpoint is the previewed VTXO outpoint, formatted as "txid:index".

fields.name: funding_address
fields.comment:
funding_address is empty when can_start is true (no shortfall, so no
address is allocated).

fields.name: required_confirmations
fields.comment:
required_confirmations is the number of confirmations a
backing-wallet UTXO must have before it can fund the unroll CPFP.

fields.name: required_fee_utxo_count
fields.comment:
required_fee_utxo_count is the number of distinct confirmed wallet
UTXOs the exit needs to fund CPFP fees, one per unroll ancestry path.

fields.name: usable_fee_utxo_count
fields.comment:
usable_fee_utxo_count is the number of confirmed wallet UTXOs large
enough to each fund a CPFP child on their own.

fields.name: recommended_utxo_amount_sat
fields.comment:
recommended_utxo_amount_sat is the suggested value in satoshis for
each fee-funding UTXO the caller provisions.

fields.name: recommended_total_funding_sat
fields.comment:
recommended_total_funding_sat is the suggested total backing-wallet
funding in satoshis: recommended_utxo_amount_sat times the required
fee UTXO count.

fields.name: funding_shortfall_sat
fields.comment:
funding_shortfall_sat is the additional backing-wallet funding in
satoshis needed before this exit can start. Zero when can_start is
true.

fields.name: can_start
fields.comment:
can_start is true when the backing wallet already has the fee inputs
and balance to fund this exit's forced unroll.

fields.name: exit_job_found
fields.comment:
exit_job_found is true when an unroll job already exists for this
outpoint, in which case exit_status, sweep_txid, and last_error
describe it.

fields.name: exit_status
fields.comment:
exit_status is the current phase of the existing unroll job, when
exit_job_found is true.

fields.name: sweep_txid
fields.comment:
sweep_txid is the hex-encoded txid of the existing job's sweep
transaction, set once it has been broadcast.

fields.name: last_error
fields.comment:
last_error is the failure reason of the existing unroll job, when it
is in a failed state.

fields.name: error
fields.comment:
error is a per-outpoint failure (e.g. VTXO not found) so one bad
outpoint does not fail the whole batch. Empty on success.

fields.name: infeasibility_reason
fields.comment:
infeasibility_reason explains why can_start is false. It may be a
structural block - a dust or uneconomical VTXO the wallet can never
make exitable (funding_shortfall_sat is zero) - or a funding
shortfall the wallet could cover (wallet underfunded or too few fee
inputs, also reflected in funding_shortfall_sat). It is
EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_UNSPECIFIED when can_start is true.

SweepWalletRequest.name: SweepWalletRequest
SweepWalletRequest.comment:
SweepWalletRequest describes a backing-wallet sweep to preview or broadcast
via WalletService.SweepWallet.

fields.name: destination_address
fields.comment:
destination_address is the Bitcoin address that receives the backing
wallet funds after fees.

fields.name: broadcast
fields.comment:
broadcast controls whether the sweep is only previewed or also signed
and published.

fields.name: fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte
fields.comment: fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte overrides chain fee estimation when positive.

fields.name: conf_target
fields.comment:
conf_target selects the fee-estimation target in blocks when
fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte is unset. Zero uses the daemon's default unroll
confirmation target.

SweepWalletResponse.name: SweepWalletResponse
SweepWalletResponse.comment:
SweepWalletResponse is the preview or broadcast result of
WalletService.SweepWallet.

fields.name: inputs
fields.comment: inputs are the confirmed backing-wallet UTXOs selected for the sweep.

fields.name: total_input_sat
fields.comment:
total_input_sat is the gross value in satoshis of every selected
input.

fields.name: estimated_fee_sat
fields.comment:
estimated_fee_sat is the absolute miner fee in satoshis for the
aggregate sweep transaction at the resolved fee rate.

fields.name: net_amount_sat
fields.comment:
net_amount_sat is total_input_sat minus estimated_fee_sat: the amount
in satoshis paid to the destination address.

fields.name: fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte
fields.comment:
fee_rate_sat_per_vbyte is the (capped) fee rate used to build the
sweep transaction.

fields.name: can_broadcast
fields.comment:
can_broadcast is true when the preview cleared the dust floor and the
sweep can be published.

fields.name: txid
fields.comment:
txid is the hex-encoded sweep transaction id, set only when the sweep
was broadcast.

fields.name: failure_reason
fields.comment:
failure_reason is populated when the sweep cannot broadcast (no
confirmed inputs, dust after fees) or the broadcast failed after
preview.

WalletSweepInput.name: WalletSweepInput
WalletSweepInput.comment: WalletSweepInput is one backing-wallet UTXO selected for a sweep.

fields.name: outpoint
fields.comment:
outpoint is the selected backing-wallet UTXO, formatted as
"txid:index".

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment: amount_sat is the value of the selected UTXO in satoshis.

ExitRequest.name: ExitRequest
ExitRequest.comment:
ExitRequest identifies the VTXO to exit and selects the exit path for
WalletService.Exit: cooperative leave by default, or unilateral unroll
when force_unroll_ack is supplied.

fields.name: outpoint
fields.comment: outpoint is the VTXO outpoint to exit, formatted as "txid:index".

fields.name: onchain_address
fields.comment:
onchain_address is the cooperative leave destination. Empty asks the
daemon to generate a fresh backing-wallet address internally.

fields.name: force_unroll_ack
fields.comment:
force_unroll_ack must be exactly "I_KNOW_WHAT_I_AM_DOING" to bypass
cooperative leave and start unilateral unroll. It cannot be combined
with onchain_address, and the server requires at least one confirmed
local backing-wallet UTXO before admitting forced unroll.

ExitResponse.name: ExitResponse
ExitResponse.comment:
ExitResponse reports the exit path the daemon took and its per-path detail
for WalletService.Exit.

fields.name: created
fields.comment:
created indicates whether a new unilateral exit job was spawned.
False if an existing job already covers this target. Cooperative
exits leave this unset.

fields.name: actor_id
fields.comment: actor_id is the identifier of the durable unilateral exit job actor.

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: mode identifies the branch the daemon took.

fields.name: queued_outpoints
fields.comment: queued_outpoints lists the outpoints accepted into cooperative leave.

fields.name: onchain_address
fields.comment:
onchain_address is the cooperative leave destination used by the
daemon. It may be caller-supplied or daemon-generated.

ExitStatusRequest.name: ExitStatusRequest
ExitStatusRequest.comment:
ExitStatusRequest identifies the VTXO whose exit job to query via
WalletService.ExitStatus.

fields.name: outpoint
fields.comment:
outpoint is the VTXO outpoint to query, formatted as
"txid:index".

fields.name: detailed
fields.comment:
detailed enriches the response with tree/CSV progress and a fee
breakdown. It costs one extra child actor round-trip plus a fee
estimate, so it should be set only for interactive status commands, not
automated polling.

ExitStatusResponse.name: ExitStatusResponse
ExitStatusResponse.comment:
ExitStatusResponse reports the current phase of an unroll job for
WalletService.ExitStatus.

fields.name: found
fields.comment: found is true if an exit job exists for the requested outpoint.

fields.name: status
fields.comment: status is the current high-level phase of the exit job.

fields.name: sweep_txid
fields.comment:
sweep_txid is the txid of the sweep transaction, set once the
sweep has been broadcast.

fields.name: last_error
fields.comment:
last_error contains the failure reason if the job is in FAILED
status.

fields.name: phase_detail
fields.comment:
phase_detail is a human-readable one-line description of the current
phase, e.g. "materializing layer 2 of 4 (3/7 txs confirmed)". Set only
on a detailed query.

fields.name: progress
fields.comment:
progress is the tree materialization progress. Set only on a detailed
query against a live job.

fields.name: csv
fields.comment:
csv is the CSV maturity countdown. Set only on a detailed query, once
the target has confirmed.

fields.name: fees
fields.comment:
fees is the on-chain cost breakdown for the exit. Set only on a detailed
query.

fields.name: best_case_blocks_remaining
fields.comment:
best_case_blocks_remaining is the optimistic block count until a
confirmed sweep. Set only on a detailed query.

fields.name: current_height
fields.comment:
current_height is the best block height the exit job has observed. Set
only on a detailed query against a live job.

ExitProgress.name: ExitProgress
ExitProgress.comment:
ExitProgress describes materialization progress through the exit proof tree.
It is populated only for a detailed query against a live exit job.

fields.name: confirmed_txs
fields.comment: confirmed_txs is the number of proof transactions confirmed on-chain.

fields.name: in_flight_txs
fields.comment:
in_flight_txs is the number of proof transactions broadcast but not yet
observed confirmed.

fields.name: ready_txs
fields.comment: ready_txs is the number of proof transactions ready to broadcast now.

fields.name: blocked_txs
fields.comment:
blocked_txs is the number of proof transactions still waiting on an
unconfirmed in-proof parent.

fields.name: total_txs
fields.comment: total_txs is the total number of transactions in the exit proof tree.

fields.name: current_layer
fields.comment:
current_layer is the frontier layer index: the shallowest topological
layer (roots first) that still holds an unconfirmed transaction.

fields.name: total_layers
fields.comment: total_layers is the depth of the exit proof tree.

fields.name: target_confirmed
fields.comment: target_confirmed is true once the target VTXO transaction has confirmed.

fields.name: all_proof_confirmed
fields.comment:
all_proof_confirmed is true once every proof-tree transaction has
confirmed, so only the CSV wait and final sweep remain.

ExitCSV.name: ExitCSV
ExitCSV.comment:
ExitCSV describes the target's CSV maturity countdown. It is populated only
once the target transaction has confirmed.

fields.name: target_confirm_height
fields.comment: target_confirm_height is the block height at which the target confirmed.

fields.name: maturity_height
fields.comment:
maturity_height is the block height at which the target becomes
timeout-spendable.

fields.name: blocks_remaining
fields.comment: blocks_remaining is how many blocks remain until CSV maturity.

fields.name: mature
fields.comment: mature is true once the current height is at or past maturity.

ExitFees.name: ExitFees
ExitFees.comment:
ExitFees breaks down the on-chain cost of the exit. The CPFP total is
estimated from the current fee rate; the sweep fee is the actual built-sweep
fee once known, otherwise estimated.

fields.name: cpfp_fee_sat
fields.comment:
cpfp_fee_sat is the wallet-funded CPFP fee across every recovery
transaction (estimated).

fields.name: sweep_fee_sat
fields.comment: sweep_fee_sat is the fee the final sweep pays out of the VTXO value.

fields.name: total_cost_sat
fields.comment: total_cost_sat is cpfp_fee_sat + sweep_fee_sat.

fields.name: vtxo_amount_sat
fields.comment: vtxo_amount_sat is the value of the VTXO being exited.

fields.name: net_recovered_sat
fields.comment: net_recovered_sat is vtxo_amount_sat - sweep_fee_sat.

fields.name: fee_rate_sat_vbyte
fields.comment: fee_rate_sat_vbyte is the fee rate used for the estimate.

fields.name: sweep_fee_actual
fields.comment:
sweep_fee_actual is true when sweep_fee_sat is the real built-sweep fee
rather than an estimate.

fields.name: spent_so_far_sat
fields.comment:
spent_so_far_sat is the estimated on-chain fee already committed at this
point in the exit: CPFP for the confirmed and in-flight recovery
transactions, plus the sweep fee once the sweep is broadcast. It is an
estimate, not a realized total; total_cost_sat remains the projected cost
of the whole exit.

ExitSummaryRequest.name: ExitSummaryRequest
ExitSummaryRequest.comment: ExitSummaryRequest asks for the wallet-wide portfolio of in-progress exits.

ExitSummaryResponse.name: ExitSummaryResponse
ExitSummaryResponse.comment:
ExitSummaryResponse is the wallet-wide portfolio of in-progress exits plus
aggregate totals. Only non-terminal exits are included: a completed or failed
exit has no amount "left" to recover.

fields.name: exits
fields.comment: exits is one row per in-progress exit.

fields.name: total_exits
fields.comment: total_exits is the number of in-progress exits.

fields.name: total_vtxo_amount_sat
fields.comment:
total_vtxo_amount_sat is the summed value of every VTXO still being
exited: the total amount currently locked in in-progress exits.

fields.name: total_est_fee_sat
fields.comment:
total_est_fee_sat is the summed projected on-chain fee across every
in-progress exit.

fields.name: total_est_net_recovered_sat
fields.comment:
total_est_net_recovered_sat is the summed estimated value that will land
back in the wallet once every in-progress exit completes.

ExitSummaryItem.name: ExitSummaryItem
ExitSummaryItem.comment:
ExitSummaryItem is one in-progress exit's coarse contribution to the
portfolio. It is intentionally cheap (phase plus amounts and estimated fees
from the persisted descriptor), so the summary does not query each live
actor; use ExitStatus --detailed for one exit's tree/CSV progress.

fields.name: outpoint
fields.comment: outpoint is the exiting VTXO outpoint, formatted as "txid:index".

fields.name: status
fields.comment: status is the current high-level phase of the exit job.

fields.name: vtxo_amount_sat
fields.comment: vtxo_amount_sat is the value of the VTXO being exited.

fields.name: est_total_fee_sat
fields.comment:
est_total_fee_sat is the projected total on-chain fee for this exit
(estimated CPFP total plus sweep fee).

fields.name: est_net_recovered_sat
fields.comment:
est_net_recovered_sat is the estimated value that lands back in the
wallet after the sweep fee.

fields.name: include_existing
fields.comment:
include_existing replays the full activity history before live
updates. It is ignored when cursor is set (cursor already selects the
replay start).

fields.name: kinds
fields.comment:
kinds optionally narrows the stream to specific entry categories.
When empty, all kinds are streamed.

fields.name: cursor
fields.comment:
cursor resumes the stream from a prior SubscribeWalletResponse.cursor:
the daemon replays every event after it, then continues live. Zero
(unset) starts from the full history when include_existing is set, or
from the current tip otherwise.

SubscribeWalletResponse.name: SubscribeWalletResponse
SubscribeWalletResponse.comment:
SubscribeWalletResponse is one item in the SubscribeWallet stream: either a
projected activity row or a gap signal telling the subscriber it fell behind
and must reconcile.

fields.name: cursor
fields.comment:
cursor is the monotonic event-log position of this update. A client
persists it and reconnects with SubscribeWalletRequest.cursor set to the
last value it processed to resume without gaps. The sequence is
monotonic but NOT contiguous: a value may be skipped, so a client treats
any cursor past its last as new and never infers a dropped event from a
gap in the numbers.

fields.name: entry
fields.comment: entry is a projected activity row at cursor.

fields.name: gap
fields.comment:
gap signals the subscriber fell behind the live buffer and should
reconcile current state via List, then resume from cursor.

SubscribeGap.name: SubscribeGap
SubscribeGap.comment:
SubscribeGap tells a subscriber the daemon could not keep it current on the
live stream (a slow consumer overflowed the send buffer). The subscriber
should reconcile via List and resume from the enclosing cursor. No activity
is lost: the canonical event log retains every event, so the resume replays
anything missed.

fields.name: reason
fields.comment: reason is a short human-readable description of why the gap was emitted.

InspectActivityRequest.name: InspectActivityRequest
InspectActivityRequest.comment: InspectActivityRequest identifies the activity entry to inspect.

fields.name: id
fields.comment:
id is the WalletEntry.id to inspect.
InspectActivity searches the daemon's current activity window, which is
capped by the daemon maximum list limit.

fields.name: ledger_limit
fields.comment:
ledger_limit caps how many ledger rows are scanned for correlation.
Zero means use the daemon's maximum list limit.

InspectActivityResponse.name: InspectActivityResponse
InspectActivityResponse.comment:
InspectActivityResponse contains the friendly activity row plus the
lower-level records that explain how the daemon derived it.

fields.name: entry
fields.comment: entry is the user-facing activity row being inspected.

fields.name: swap
fields.comment: swap is populated when the entry is backed by a pay or receive swap.

fields.name: vtxos
fields.comment:
vtxos contains best-effort VTXO movements correlated to this
activity. Rows are derived from local swap summaries and ledger
accounting, so ids may be OOR session/output ids when a full VTXO
outpoint is not persisted by the source row.

fields.name: ledger_rows
fields.comment:
ledger_rows are the local accounting rows used to build the trace,
including rows hidden from the friendly activity feed.

fields.name: notes
fields.comment: notes contains plain-English caveats about best-effort correlation.

ActivitySwapTrace.name: ActivitySwapTrace
ActivitySwapTrace.comment:
ActivitySwapTrace is the swap-service snapshot correlated to one wallet
activity entry.

fields.name: payment_hash
fields.comment:
payment_hash is the Lightning payment hash that identifies the
swap-backed activity.

fields.name: direction
fields.comment:
direction is the raw swapclientrpc.SwapDirection enum name, kept as a
string so this debug surface can expose swap state without importing the
swap service proto into the wallet proto.

fields.name: state
fields.comment: state is the raw swapclientrpc.SwapState enum name at inspection time.

fields.name: pending
fields.comment: pending is true while the backing swap state machine is still active.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment:
amount_sat is the swap amount in satoshis using the swap service's
unsigned amount convention.

fields.name: fee_sat
fields.comment: fee_sat is the fee reported by the swap service for this swap.

fields.name: invoice
fields.comment: invoice is the BOLT-11 invoice associated with the swap when known.

fields.name: vhtlc_outpoint
fields.comment: vhtlc_outpoint is the Ark vHTLC output observed for the swap when known.

fields.name: vhtlc_amount_sat
fields.comment: vhtlc_amount_sat is the amount held by vhtlc_outpoint when known.

fields.name: funding_session_id
fields.comment: funding_session_id is the OOR session that funded the swap when known.

fields.name: claim_session_id
fields.comment: claim_session_id is the OOR session that claimed the swap when known.

fields.name: refund_session_id
fields.comment: refund_session_id is the OOR session that refunded the swap when known.

fields.name: terminal_reason
fields.comment:
terminal_reason is the swap service's terminal reason string when the
swap reached a failed or intervention state.

fields.name: created_at_unix
fields.comment: created_at_unix is the swap creation timestamp in unix seconds.

fields.name: updated_at_unix
fields.comment: updated_at_unix is the last swap update timestamp in unix seconds.

fields.name: deadline_unix
fields.comment:
deadline_unix is the swap deadline in unix seconds when the swap service
exposes one.

fields.name: refund_locktime
fields.comment:
refund_locktime is the absolute locktime after which the swap can be
refunded.

fields.name: settlement_type
fields.comment:
settlement_type is the raw swapclientrpc.SwapSettlementType enum name
when known.

fields.name: sender_pubkey
fields.comment: sender_pubkey is the compressed SEC-encoded vHTLC sender key when known.

fields.name: preimage
fields.comment:
preimage is the hex-encoded Lightning payment preimage once the swap
revealed it. For a completed pay swap this is the proof of payment for
the paid invoice; it is empty until the preimage is durably known.

ActivityVTXOTrace.name: ActivityVTXOTrace
ActivityVTXOTrace.comment:
ActivityVTXOTrace describes one VTXO movement correlated to a wallet activity
entry.

fields.name: id
fields.comment:
id is the best available identifier. It is an outpoint when the
source has one, otherwise an OOR session/output identifier.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment: amount_sat is the VTXO amount in satoshis.

fields.name: role
fields.comment:
role is a short technical label such as "spent_input",
"change_output", "materialized_output", or "vhtlc_output".

fields.name: ours
fields.comment: ours is true when the row represents wallet-owned funds.

fields.name: source
fields.comment:
source names the local source used to derive the row, such as
"ledger" or "swap".

fields.name: session_id
fields.comment: session_id is the OOR session/correlation id when known.

fields.name: output_index
fields.comment:
output_index is set when the source names a specific OOR output
index.

ActivityLedgerTrace.name: ActivityLedgerTrace
ActivityLedgerTrace.comment:
ActivityLedgerTrace describes one local ledger row correlated to a wallet
activity entry.

fields.name: source
fields.comment:
source names the subsystem that produced the row, such as "ledger" or
"boarding_sweep".

fields.name: type
fields.comment: type is the broad transaction group used by the wallet history query.

fields.name: subtype
fields.comment: subtype is the ledger event type or sweep state.

fields.name: amount_sat
fields.comment:
amount_sat is the row amount in satoshis using the ledger sign
convention.

fields.name: fee_sat
fields.comment:
fee_sat is the row's fee contribution in satoshis when the history query
can derive one.

fields.name: created_at_unix
fields.comment: created_at_unix is the row creation timestamp in unix seconds.

fields.name: confirmation_status
fields.comment:
confirmation_status is the best-known chain confirmation state for the
row, for example "pending", "confirmed", or "recorded".

fields.name: description
fields.comment: description is the local ledger description persisted with the row.

fields.name: entry_id
fields.comment: entry_id is the local ledger row id when the source is the ledger.

fields.name: txid
fields.comment: txid is the associated chain transaction id when known.

fields.name: debit_account
fields.comment: debit_account is the local accounting debit account.

fields.name: credit_account
fields.comment: credit_account is the local accounting credit account.

fields.name: round_id
fields.comment: round_id is the Ark round id associated with the row when known.

fields.name: session_id
fields.comment:
session_id is the OOR session/correlation id associated with the row when
known.

fields.name: confirmation_height
fields.comment: confirmation_height is the confirmed block height when known.

fields.name: hidden_from_activity
fields.comment:
hidden_from_activity is true when WalletService.List suppresses the
row because it is an internal execution leg.

fields.name: role
fields.comment: role is the inspection role inferred for this row.

fields.name: output_index
fields.comment:
output_index is the transaction output index associated with txid.
A negative value means the source has no output index.

SendRail.name: SendRail
SendRail.comment: SendRail identifies the expected settlement rail for a prepared send.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
SEND_RAIL_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value used when no rail
has been determined.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_OFFCHAIN_UNKNOWN
values.comment:
SEND_RAIL_OFFCHAIN_UNKNOWN is used when the wallet can parse the
invoice locally but the swapserver has not supplied a quote that
distinguishes same-Ark settlement from Lightning settlement.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_IN_ARK
values.comment:
SEND_RAIL_IN_ARK is same-Ark peer-to-peer settlement, where the
payment never touches Lightning.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_LIGHTNING
values.comment: SEND_RAIL_LIGHTNING is settlement over the real Lightning network.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_ONCHAIN
values.comment:
SEND_RAIL_ONCHAIN is an on-chain send via a cooperative leave to
the destination address.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_CREDIT
values.comment:
SEND_RAIL_CREDIT is settlement drawn from the wallet's
server-side credit balance.

values.name: SEND_RAIL_MIXED
values.comment:
SEND_RAIL_MIXED is settlement that combines credit with the
normal vHTLC path.

SendQuoteStatus.name: SendQuoteStatus
SendQuoteStatus.comment: SendQuoteStatus describes how complete the prepare-time quote is.

values.name: SEND_QUOTE_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
SEND_QUOTE_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value; quote
completeness was not reported.

values.name: SEND_QUOTE_STATUS_COMPLETE
values.comment:
SEND_QUOTE_STATUS_COMPLETE means every user-visible amount and fee
field is backed by a remote quote.

values.name: SEND_QUOTE_STATUS_LOCAL_ONLY
values.comment:
SEND_QUOTE_STATUS_LOCAL_ONLY means the wallet performed local
validation and selection but one or more remote quote APIs are not
available yet.

EntryKind.name: EntryKind
EntryKind.comment:
EntryKind tags each WalletEntry with the user-visible category of the
underlying operation. Internal subtypes (same-Ark p2p vs real Lightning,
OOR session correlators, round IDs) are deliberately not surfaced.

values.name: ENTRY_KIND_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
ENTRY_KIND_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value used when the
operation category is unknown.

values.name: ENTRY_KIND_SEND
values.comment:
ENTRY_KIND_SEND is an outbound payment over any rail (Lightning,
same-Ark, onchain, or credit).

values.name: ENTRY_KIND_RECV
values.comment: ENTRY_KIND_RECV is an inbound receive.

values.name: ENTRY_KIND_DEPOSIT
values.comment: ENTRY_KIND_DEPOSIT is a boarding deposit rolled into a VTXO.

values.name: ENTRY_KIND_EXIT
values.comment:
ENTRY_KIND_EXIT is a cooperative leave or unilateral exit to
on-chain funds.

EntryStatus.name: EntryStatus
EntryStatus.comment:
EntryStatus collapses every backing FSM into the three states user-facing
surfaces actually need.

values.name: ENTRY_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
ENTRY_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value used when the
backing state has not been mapped yet.

values.name: ENTRY_STATUS_PENDING
values.comment: ENTRY_STATUS_PENDING means the operation is still in flight.

values.name: ENTRY_STATUS_COMPLETE
values.comment:
ENTRY_STATUS_COMPLETE means the operation reached a durable
success state.

values.name: ENTRY_STATUS_FAILED
values.comment:
ENTRY_STATUS_FAILED means the operation reached a terminal
failure state.

WalletEntryPhase.name: WalletEntryPhase
WalletEntryPhase.comment:
WalletEntryPhase is a coarse, wallet-facing lifecycle phase. It is not a
replacement for status; status answers whether the operation is pending,
complete, or failed, while phase explains the current backing-system step.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value used when the
backing subsystem has no lifecycle hint.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_REQUEST_CREATED
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_REQUEST_CREATED means the request was created but no
payment has been observed.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT means the wallet is waiting for an
inbound payment or swap funding.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_PAYMENT_DETECTED
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_PAYMENT_DETECTED means the backing subsystem has
detected a payment but it is not yet settled.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_SETTLING
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_SETTLING means the operation is being settled through
Ark, Lightning, or onchain machinery.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_CONFIRMED
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_CONFIRMED means the backing operation is confirmed or
otherwise durably complete.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_REFUNDING
values.comment: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_REFUNDING means the operation is currently refunding.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_REFUNDED
values.comment: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_REFUNDED means the refund path completed.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_FAILED
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_FAILED means the backing operation reached a terminal
failed state.

values.name: WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_WAITING_FOR_CONFIRMATION
values.comment:
WALLET_ENTRY_PHASE_WAITING_FOR_CONFIRMATION means an onchain payment has
been detected and is waiting for block confirmation.

EntryFailureCode.name: EntryFailureCode
EntryFailureCode.comment:
EntryFailureCode is a stable, machine-readable classification of why a FAILED
WalletEntry failed. It complements the free-text failure_reason so clients
can branch on failure cause without string matching.

values.name: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_UNSPECIFIED is the zero value used for non-failed
entries or when the cause is unknown.

values.name: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_TIMED_OUT
values.comment:
ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_TIMED_OUT means the operation exceeded the wallet
deadline before reaching a terminal state.

values.name: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_EXPIRED
values.comment: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_EXPIRED means the swap expired before it was funded.

values.name: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_REFUNDED
values.comment:
ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_REFUNDED means an outbound payment was refunded back
to the wallet.

values.name: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_NEEDS_INTERVENTION
values.comment:
ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_NEEDS_INTERVENTION means the swap reached an anomalous
state requiring manual recovery.

values.name: ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_FAILED
values.comment:
ENTRY_FAILURE_CODE_FAILED is a generic terminal failure with no more
specific classification.

ListView.name: ListView
ListView.comment:
ListView selects which slice of wallet state List returns. The default
(LIST_VIEW_UNSPECIFIED) is treated as LIST_VIEW_ACTIVITY for backwards
feel: callers that don't care about the new shape keep getting the
activity stream.

values.name: LIST_VIEW_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
LIST_VIEW_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value; List treats it as
LIST_VIEW_ACTIVITY.

values.name: LIST_VIEW_ACTIVITY
values.comment:
LIST_VIEW_ACTIVITY returns the merged WalletEntry stream (send /
recv / deposit / exit) across the swap subsystem, OOR sessions, the
boarding ledger, and the unroll registry.

values.name: LIST_VIEW_VTXOS
values.comment:
LIST_VIEW_VTXOS returns the live VTXO inventory (one row per
spendable VTXO).

values.name: LIST_VIEW_ONCHAIN
values.comment:
LIST_VIEW_ONCHAIN returns the on-chain transaction history
(boarding deposits, boarding sweeps, leave outputs, round
commitment txs that confirmed against this wallet).

ExitJobStatus.name: ExitJobStatus
ExitJobStatus.comment:
ExitJobStatus collapses the underlying unroll job phases to a short
wallet-facing string set.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED - the proto zero value; no job phase
has been reported.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_PENDING
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_PENDING - job created but recovery transactions
not yet materialized.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_MATERIALIZING
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_MATERIALIZING - recovery transactions are being
broadcast and confirmed on-chain.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_CSV_PENDING
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_CSV_PENDING - recovery transactions confirmed,
waiting for the CSV delay to expire.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_SWEEPING
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_SWEEPING - CSV delay expired, sweep transaction
is being broadcast/confirmed.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_COMPLETED
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_COMPLETED - terminal: the sweep confirmed and the
funds are in the on-chain wallet.

values.name: EXIT_JOB_STATUS_FAILED
values.comment:
EXIT_JOB_STATUS_FAILED - terminal: an unrecoverable error
occurred.

ExitInfeasibilityReason.name: ExitInfeasibilityReason
ExitInfeasibilityReason.comment:
ExitInfeasibilityReason enumerates why a unilateral exit was judged
infeasible. It mirrors the daemon's unroll feasibility verdict so a
caller can distinguish a dust/uneconomical block (no amount of wallet
funding fixes it) from a plain funding shortfall.

values.name: EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_UNSPECIFIED - the proto zero value; the
exit is feasible, or the block is a plain funding shortfall reported
via funding_shortfall_sat instead.

values.name: EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_SWEEP_BELOW_DUST
values.comment:
EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_SWEEP_BELOW_DUST - the swept output, after
deducting the sweep fee from the VTXO value, would fall at or below
the dust limit, so the sweep could never relay. The exit is
impossible regardless of wallet funding.

values.name: EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_UNECONOMICAL
values.comment:
EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_UNECONOMICAL - the total on-chain cost to
recover the VTXO exceeds the configured fraction of its value. The
exit could technically complete but burns more than it returns.

values.name: EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_WALLET_UNDERFUNDED
values.comment:
EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_WALLET_UNDERFUNDED - the confirmed on-chain
wallet balance is too small to cover the CPFP fees. Funding the
wallet and retrying resolves it (also reported via
funding_shortfall_sat).

values.name: EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_WALLET_TOO_FEW_INPUTS
values.comment:
EXIT_INFEASIBILITY_REASON_WALLET_TOO_FEW_INPUTS - the wallet has
fewer usable confirmed UTXOs than the VTXO has independent ancestry
paths, so it lacks distinct CPFP fee inputs.

ExitMode.name: ExitMode
ExitMode.comment:
ExitMode identifies whether Exit queued a cooperative leave or started a
forced unilateral unroll.

values.name: EXIT_MODE_UNSPECIFIED
values.comment:
EXIT_MODE_UNSPECIFIED is the proto zero value used when the exit
path was not reported.

values.name: EXIT_MODE_COOPERATIVE
values.comment:
EXIT_MODE_COOPERATIVE means Exit queued a cooperative leave
(VTXO-to-onchain).

values.name: EXIT_MODE_UNILATERAL
values.comment:
EXIT_MODE_UNILATERAL means Exit started a forced unilateral
unroll.
